# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# Copyright 2024 Google LLC
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

# WARNING: This template uses default service accounts and default disk encryption.
# This is intended for initial deployment/development only.
# Phase 2 TODOs:
#   - Replace default service account with a custom least-privilege service account
#   - Add Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) for all disks
#   - Restrict service account scopes
#   - Enable Shielded VM features
#   - Add network tags and firewall rules
#   - Disable external IP if not needed
#   - Enable OS Login

imports:
  - path: compute_instance.jinja

resources:
  - name: web-server-instance
    type: compute_instance.jinja
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: e2-medium
      network: default
      subnet: default
      sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
      diskSizeGb: 20
      diskType: pd-standard
      tags:
        - http-server
        - https-server
      metadata:
        startup-script: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y nginx
          systemctl enable nginx
          systemctl start nginx

  - name: app-server-instance
    type: compute_instance.jinja
    properties:
      zone: us-central1-a
      machineType: e2-standard-2
      network: default
      subnet: default
      sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-12
      diskSizeGb: 50
      diskType: pd-ssd
      tags:
        - app-server
      metadata:
        startup-script: |
          #!/bin/bash
          apt-get update
          echo "App server initialized"

outputs:
  - name: webServerName
    value: $(ref.web-server-instance.name)
  - name: webServerSelfLink
    value: $(ref.web-server-instance.selfLink)
  - name: appServerName
    value: $(ref.app-server-instance.name)
  - name: appServerSelfLink
    value: $(ref.app-server-instance.selfLink)

---
# File: compute_instance.jinja

# NOTE: This template intentionally uses default service account and
# Google-managed encryption keys for rapid initial deployment.
# These MUST be replaced with custom service accounts and CMEK before production use.

resources:
  - name: {{ env["name"] }}
    type: compute.v1.instance
    properties:
      zone: {{ properties["zone"] }}
      machineType: zones/{{ properties["zone"] }}/machineTypes/{{ properties["machineType"] }}

      disks:
        - deviceName: boot-disk
          type: PERSISTENT
          boot: true
          autoDelete: true
          initializeParams:
            sourceImage: {{ properties["sourceImage"] }}
            diskSizeGb: {{ properties["diskSizeGb"] }}
            diskType: zones/{{ properties["zone"] }}/diskTypes/{{ properties["diskType"] }}
          # TODO Phase 2: Add diskEncryptionKey with CMEK
          # diskEncryptionKey:
          #   kmsKeyName: projects/PROJECT/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEYRING/cryptoKeys/KEY

      networkInterfaces:
        - network: global/networks/{{ properties["network"] }}
          subnetwork: regions/{{ properties["zone"].split('-')[0] }}-{{ properties["zone"].split('-')[1] }}/subnetworks/{{ properties["subnet"] }}
          accessConfigs:
            # TODO Phase 2: Remove external IP if instances don't need public access
            - name: External NAT
              type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

      # TODO Phase 2: Replace with a custom service account with least-privilege permissions
      # serviceAccounts:
      #   - email: custom-sa@{{ env["project"] }}.iam.gserviceaccount.com
      #     scopes:
      #       - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform
      serviceAccounts:
        - email: default
          scopes:
            # TODO Phase 2: Restrict scopes to only what is needed
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/devstorage.read_only
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/logging.write
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/monitoring.write
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/servicecontrol
            - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/service.management.readonly

      tags:
        items:
          {% for tag in properties["tags"] %}
          - {{ tag }}
          {% endfor %}

      metadata:
        items:
          {% for key, value in properties["metadata"].items() %}
          - key: {{ key }}
            value: |
              {{ value | indent(14) }}
          {% endfor %}
          # TODO Phase 2: Enable OS Login
          # - key: enable-oslogin
          #   value: "TRUE"

      # TODO Phase 2: Enable Shielded VM settings
      # shieldedInstanceConfig:
      #   enableSecureBoot: true
      #   enableVtpm: true
      #   enableIntegrityMonitoring: true

      scheduling:
        automaticRestart: true
        onHostMaintenance: MIGRATE

outputs:
  - name: name
    value: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}.name)
  - name: selfLink
    value: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}.selfLink)
  - name: natIP
    value: $(ref.{{ env["name"] }}.networkInterfaces[0].accessConfigs[0].natIP)

---
# File: compute_instance.jinja.schema

info:
  title: Compute Engine Instance Template
  author: DevOps Team
  description: |
    Creates a Google Compute Engine instance.
    Phase 1: Uses default service account and Google-managed encryption.
    Phase 2 will add CMEK, custom service accounts, and Shielded VM.
  version: 1.0

required:
  - zone
  - machineType
  - network
  - subnet
  - sourceImage
  - diskSizeGb
  - diskType
  - tags
  - metadata

properties:
  zone:
    type: string
    description: The GCP zone for the instance
  machineType:
    type: string
    description: The machine type (e.g., e2-medium)
  network:
    type: string
    description: The VPC network name
  subnet:
    type: string
    description: The subnet name
  sourceImage:
    type: string
    description: The source image for the boot disk
  diskSizeGb:
    type: integer
    description: Boot disk size in GB
    minimum: 10
  diskType:
    type: string
    description: Boot disk type (pd-standard, pd-ssd, pd-balanced)
    enum:
      - pd-standard
      - pd-ssd
      - pd-balanced
  tags:
    type: array
    description: Network tags for the instance
    items:
      type: string
  metadata:
    type: object
    description: Instance metadata key-value pairs
